items.title: S&P 500 closes higher as chip stocks rise and oil prices slide
items.content: S&P 500 rose 0.81% to 7,543.64. Dow added 0.27% to 52,487.41. Nasdaq gained 1.30% to 26,206.89. Chip stocks led recovery as oil pulled back from geopolitical highs.

items.title: Dow drops below 53K; Nasdaq falls 1% as chip stocks tumble
items.content: Dow declined 130.76 points to 52,925.15. S&P 500 down 0.45% to 7,503.85. Nasdaq dropped 1.16% to 25,818.69 as chip stocks fell and oil spiked on Iran escalation.

items.title: Dow Jones rises on Cisco, Goldman strength
items.content: Dow rose 96 points (+0.18%) to close at 52,444 on July 9. Cisco Systems led (+3.77%), followed by American Express (+2.94%) and Goldman Sachs (+2.51%).

items.title: Hong Kong stocks surge 3% as tech lock-up expiry fears ease
items.content: Hang Seng Index closed up 3%. Tech Index surged ~5% to 4,731. Zhipu AI gained 13.4%, MiniMax +12%, Alibaba +12%, Xiaomi +9%. Lock-up expiries absorbed without sell-off.

items.title: Hang Seng slips 0.70% to 24,030 as tech volatility returns
items.content: HSI fell 169.28 points to 24,030.18 on July 9, reversing part of Wednesday's 3% rally. Tech volatility and busy IPO pipeline weighed on sentiment.

items.title: Hang Seng rocketed 5.8% on July 8
items.content: HSI surged 1,327.81 points (+5.8%) to close at 24,208.83 on July 8, the biggest single-day gain in recent memory, driven by tech and AI stocks.

items.title: May PCE inflation surges to 4.1%, highest since April 2023
items.content: Core PCE hit 3.4% annual rate (highest since Oct 2023). Headline PCE 4.1%. Monthly PCE rose 0.4%. Energy prices driven by Iran conflict continue to seep into other sectors. Fed hike in September still expected.

items.title: US and Iran threaten return to war after fiercest exchange since ceasefire
items.content: US struck 80 targets around Strait of Hormuz after Iran attacked 3 commercial vessels. Iran responded with attacks on US sites in Bahrain and Kuwait. Trump threatened to take Kharg Island. Oil prices spiked sharply.

items.title: China chip and AI stocks offset inflation-led sector splits
items.content: Mainland semiconductor shares climbed 3.1%, STAR 50 rose 3.2%. CXMT plans July 15 Shanghai IPO targeting 29.5B yuan. China June CPI cooled while PPI firmed, creating tug-of-war across sectors.

items.title: Macquarie says now 'best time' to buy Chinese AI chip stocks
items.content: Macquarie analyst team says PRC government support for domestic AI chip firms and US export controls on Nvidia GPUs have lifted growth visibility of Chinese domestic leaders like Huawei Ascend chips.

items.title: Tencent sells 273M Kuaishou shares; Kling AI backed with $2.8B
items.content: Tencent sold 273M shares in Kuaishou at 6% discount. Tencent also backed a $2.8B financing round for Kuaishou's Kling AI, showing continued AI investment appetite.

items.title: May US PCE inflation tops 4%, leaves Fed hike on the table
items.content: PCE inflation measure tops 4% annual rate. Consumer spending still strong (+0.7%). New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh committed to bringing inflation down. September rate hike remains on the table.
